---
title: 'catalog init'
---

Create a new catalog repository. The destination is selected via the
[catalog group](../index.qmd) options (`-n/--name` or `-p/--path`), which must
be supplied **before** the `init` subcommand.

## Usage

```bash
xorq catalog [-n <name> | -p <path>] init [OPTIONS]
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--env-file` | none | Path to an env file describing an annex remote (e.g. `.env.catalog.s3`). Mutually exclusive with `--env-prefix`. |
| `--env-prefix` | none | Env-var prefix describing an annex remote (e.g. `XORQ_CATALOG_S3_`). Mutually exclusive with `--env-file`. |
| `--gcs` | off | Apply GCS defaults to S3 remote config. |
| `--remote-url` | none | Git remote URL for the new catalog (sets `origin`). |

## Examples

Create a catalog at the default location:
```bash
xorq catalog --name analytics init
```

Create at an explicit path with a git remote:
```bash
xorq catalog --path ./catalogs/analytics init \
    --remote-url git@github.com:acme/analytics-catalog.git
```

Create with an S3-backed annex remote sourced from an env file:
```bash
xorq catalog --name analytics init --env-file .env.catalog.s3
```

## Notes

- `init` will refuse to overwrite an existing catalog at the target path.
- Most other catalog subcommands accept the same `-n/-p` selectors at the group
  level to point at a specific catalog.

## See also

- [`catalog clone`](clone.qmd) — clone an existing catalog from a URL
- [`catalog default`](default.qmd) — manage the default catalog name
